headline: Is Tesla (TSLA) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Pullback?
summary: If you are wondering whether Tesla's current share price still reflects a good deal or too much optimism, the key is to separate story from numbers. Over the last year Tesla has returned 36.8%, while the share price shows a 3.7% decline over 7 days, 7.5% over 30 days, and 13.1% year to date from a last close of US$380.78. Recent coverage has focused on Tesla's position as a major electric vehicle manufacturer and its role in broader conversations about growth stocks and market volatility...

headline: European car sales rise modestly in February, Tesla reverses skid
summary:
STORY: New car sales in Europe rose in February after falling in January.

And sustained demand for electric vehicles helped Tesla achieve growth for the first time since 2024.

Data from the European auto lobby the ACEA showed overall car registrations, a proxy for sales, in the EU, Britain and the European Free Trade Association were up 1.7%.

Over 979,000 cars were sold in the month, with two-thirds of those being electric or hybrid. 

The EU and Britain are walking back some regulations aimed at reducing CO2 emissions.

That's after pressure from domestic carmakers who say they are struggling to turn a profit on EV sales...

while facing competition from Chinese rivals.

But battery-electric and plug-in hybrid sales have been steadily on the rise in Europe thanks to new, cheaper models.

The data showed Tesla's February registrations were up 11.8% year-on-year ...

They were marginally lower than those of its Chinese competitor BYD, whose sales more than doubled from the same month of 2025.

Sales of top domestic carmakers VW and Stellantis rose by 2.2% and 9.5%, respectively.

While Renault's fell by 14.3%.

While Renault's fell by 14.3%.

headline: Zoox to widen US robotaxi footprint with San Francisco, Vegas expansion
summary: Zoox, Amazon's self-driving unit, said on Tuesday it will expand its robotaxi ‌service in San Francisco and Las Vegas, ‌and begin testing its purpose-built robotaxis in Austin and Miami, marking ​its broadest push yet into the U.S. autonomous ride-hailing market.  Alphabet's Waymo leads the robotaxi market, while Tesla has the financial resources and manufacturing scale to ‌expand its limited service ⁠in Austin, Texas, and plans to ramp up rapidly across the United States ⁠this year.  Meanwhile, Zoox plans to significantly grow its footprint in San Francisco, focusing on dense, high-demand neighborhoods ​such as ​the Marina, Chinatown and ​the Embarcadero, while expanding ‌in Las Vegas to cover more hotels and entertainment destinations along the Strip.

content.title: Zoox to widen US robotaxi footprint with San Francisco, Vegas expansion

Alphabet's Waymo leads the robotaxi market, while Tesla has the financial resources and manufacturing scale to ‌expand its limited service ⁠in Austin, Texas, and plans to ramp up rapidly across the United States ⁠this year.  Meanwhile, Zoox plans to significantly grow its footprint in San Francisco, focusing on dense, high-demand neighborhoods ​such as ​the Marina, Chinatown and ​the Embarcadero, while expanding ‌in Las Vegas to cover more hotels and entertainment destinations along the Strip.

headline: BYD Registrations In Europe Outpace Tesla. Here's How The Stocks Reacted.
summary: Tesla turned around its performance in Europe in February.  In February, new vehicle registrations for Tesla's rose 11.8%, after having fallen 17% the previous month, according to data from the European Automobile Manufacturers Association (ACEA) released Tuesday.  Meanwhile, the Chinese EV maker BYD saw new vehicle registrations rise 162.3% in February.

headline: Tesla's Euro sales rebound, Jefferies stock rises on SMFG reports
summary: Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland takes a closer look at some of Tuesday morning's trending tickers and stories. Jefferies (JEF) stock is moving higher after the Financial Times reported that Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group (SMFG) is weighing a buyout of the firm. Bloomberg later reported that SMFG has no immediate takeover plans. Estée Lauder (EL) is in talks to merge with Spanish beauty brand Puig (PUIG.MC). Tesla's (TSLA) European sales are rebounding. The company reported a monthly sales increase in the region for the first time in over a year.

content.title: Tesla's Euro sales rebound, Jefferies stock rises on SMFG reports

Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland takes a closer look at some of Tuesday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Jefferies (JEF) stock is moving higher after the Financial Times reported that Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group (SMFG) is weighing a buyout of the firm. Bloomberg later reported that SMFG has no immediate takeover plans.

Estée Lauder (EL) is in talks to merge with Spanish beauty brand Puig (PUIG.MC).

Tesla's (TSLA) European sales are rebounding. The company reported a monthly sales increase in the region for the first time in over a year.

headline: Gary Black Warns Tesla-SpaceX Merger Could Reduce TSLA Value By 25%: '...Looking For A Problem
summary: Investor Gary Black, who is the managing director of The Future Fund LLC, has warned against a merger between Tesla Inc. and SpaceX, sharing concerns about the effects of such a move on Tesla investors. Gary Black’s Dilution Concerns Taking to the social media platform X on Sunday, the investor shared his concerns with the possible merger, directing his message at Tesla bulls. headline: Fmr. Tesla President Talks New Book on Musk's Success

He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick on "The Close."

headline: Stocks to Watch Recap: Tesla, KB Home, Jefferies, Estée Lauder
